title: Coreopsis occidentalis
intro: Coreopsis occidentalis, also known as western tickseed, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce oxidative stress. Coreopsis occidentalis contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Coreopsis occidentalis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Coreopsis, which includes several species of coreopsis flowers. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. Coreopsis occidentalis is native to North America and is often found growing in dry areas. The classification of Coreopsis occidentalis is further broken down into kingdom Plantae and class Magnoliopsida.
